perturbation.original_text: ሺaሻ " Fintricity " means Fintricity and Fintricity Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: ሺaሻ " Fintricity " means Fintricity, its subsidiaries, and any entities it chooses to designate as Fintricity Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of 'Fintricity.' The original definition strictly includes Fintricity and its Affiliates. The modified definition allows Fintricity to arbitrarily designate other entities as 'Affiliates,' giving Fintricity discretion to extend or deny NDA protections based on its own choices. This contradicts the rest of the agreement, particularly section 13, which relies on a consistent, objective definition of 'Affiliate' to define the scope of non-solicitation obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company agrees that, during the term of this agreement and for a period of 48 months thereafter, the Company shall not, whether by itself, its officers, personnel, contractors or agents of the Company or whether by a Company A f f i l i a t e o r t h e o f f i c e r s , p e r s o n n e l , c o n t r a c t o r s o r a g e n t s o f that Company Affiliates, solicit or seek to solicit orders from any customer of Fintricity for services similar to the services which Fintricity provides to t hat customer without the prior written consent of Fintricity.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'Fintricity' from a fixed entity (Fintricity and Fintricity Affiliates) to one where Fintricity can arbitrarily designate affiliates introduces a contradiction. The original definition provided a concrete scope for the non-solicitation obligations in the contradicted text. The new definition allows Fintricity to manipulate this scope, creating uncertainty and potentially rendering the non-solicitation clause unenforceable, as it is now based on Fintricity's discretion rather than an objective definition. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as the change creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: ሺbሻ " FINTRICITY Affiliate " means any entity, whether incorporated or not, over which FINTRICITY has Control. ሺcሻ “Company Affiliate” means any entity, whether incorporated or not, over which the Company has Control. ሺdሻ " Control" ሺor variants of itሻ means the ability whether directly or i n d i r e c t l y t o d i r e c t t h e a f f a i r s o f a n o t h e r b y m e a n s o f o w n e r s h ip, contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: ሺbሻ " FINTRICITY Affiliate " means any entity, whether incorporated or not, that Fintricity deems to be an affiliate. ሺcሻ “Company Affiliate” means any entity, whether incorporated or not, that the Company considers an affiliate. ሺdሻ " Control" ሺor variants of itሻ means influence, either direct or indirect, over the other entity.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'FINTRICITY Affiliate', 'Company Affiliate' and 'Control', we introduce an in-text contradiction. Initially, 'Affiliate' was objectively determined by 'Control,' defined as the ability to direct affairs through ownership or contract. The new definition makes affiliate status subjective and the definition of control is loosely defined, based on what each company deems. This contradicts Clause 3, which outlines obligations regarding confidential information. If affiliate status and level of control is ambiguous, it's unclear which entities are bound by Clause 3, creating uncertainty in enforcing confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'FINTRICITY Affiliate', 'Company Affiliate', and 'Control' objectively based on the ability to direct affairs through ownership or contract. The changed text makes affiliate status subjective and control loosely defined, based on what each company deems. This contradicts Clause 3 (Contradicted Text), which outlines obligations regarding confidential information. The change introduces ambiguity regarding which entities are bound by Clause 3, creating uncertainty in enforcing confidentiality because the definition of 'affiliate' and 'control' is no longer objectively determined, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5 All material containing Confidential Information furnished by o r obtained from the disclosing party, including without limitation, magnetic ta pes, documents, manuals, specifications, flowcharts, program listings and data file printouts, engineering drawings, architects plans, planning documents, ሺ"t he Materials"ሻ, s h a l l b e a n d r e m a i n t h e p r o p e r t y o f t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y a n d s h all not be reproduced in whole or part without the disclosing party's expr ess written NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Page 4 of 6 © 2019 Fintricity Group – Private and Confidential c o n s e n t . A n y c o p i e s o f t h e M a t e r i a l s s h a l l b e c o m e t h e d i s c l o s i n g party's property and shall contain such copyright and other proprietary rights notice or legend as appears on the original copy.
perturbation.changed_text: 5 All material containing Confidential Information furnished by o r obtained from the disclosing party, including without limitation, magnetic ta pes, documents, manuals, specifications, flowcharts, program listings and data file printouts, engineering drawings, architects plans, planning documents, ሺ"t he Materials"ሻ, s h a l l b e a n d r e m a i n t h e p r o p e r t y o f t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y a n d m a y b e reproduced in whole or part with or without the disclosing party's expr ess written NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Page 4 of 6 © 2019 Fintricity Group – Private and Confidential c o n s e n t . A n y c o p i e s o f t h e M a t e r i a l s s h a l l b e c o m e t h e d i s c l o s i n g party's property and shall contain such copyright and other proprietary rights notice or legend as appears on the original copy.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction within Clause 5. The original text states that 'Materials' shall not be reproduced without express written consent. The modification changes this to state that the material may be reproduced with or without express written consent, giving the receiving party the power to reproduce the material even if it goes against the will of the disclosing party. This directly contradicts the first half of the sentence. This ambiguity impacts Clause 9, which requires the return or destruction of all materials upon termination. If materials can be reproduced without consent, it undermines the purpose of returning or destroying them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 5 modifies 'shall not be reproduced' to 'may be reproduced', which directly contradicts the original restriction on reproduction without consent. This creates a conflict with the contradicted text, specifically Clause 9, which requires the return or destruction of materials. If materials can be reproduced freely as stated in the changed text, the obligation to return or destroy them becomes meaningless, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9 Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 9 Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient may choose to deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. There is no request or completion of the Business Purpose in order to certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall promptly deliver up' to 'may choose to deliver up' and by omitting the part about 'request or completion of the Business Purpose' we are introducing an in-text contradiction. The original text mandates the return of materials. The modified text makes it optional. Furthermore, deleting the term to deliver a writing that 'request or completion of the Business Purpose' impacts the compliance to the obligations. This contradicts Clause 3, which outlines obligations to maintain confidentiality. If materials are not reliably returned or destroyed, the disclosing party's ability to enforce confidentiality is compromised. By retaining the materials without an obligation to certify in writing, it causes uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Clause 9 by making the return of confidential materials optional ('may choose to deliver up' instead of 'shall promptly deliver up') and removing the requirement to certify compliance. This directly contradicts Clause 3(a), which mandates the recipient to treat Confidential Information as confidential. By removing the obligation to return materials, the recipient's ability to enforce confidentiality is compromised. The contradiction is strong because Clause 3 requires confidentiality, and Clause 9, in its modified form, undermines the ability to ensure that confidentiality, based on the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10 The initial contact persons and/ or the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party shall be respected and honoured at all times and no attempt or hint of circumvention will be permitted by either of the parties, their respective offices, agents employees, assignees, guarantors or other associated party that comes to light at a latter stage.
perturbation.changed_text: 10 The initial contact persons and/ or the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party should be respected and honoured unless circumstances dictate otherwise and circumvention will be permitted at the discretion of either of the parties, their respective offices, agents employees, assignees, guarantors or other associated party that comes to light at a latter stage.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration introduces an in-text contradiction. The original Clause 10 mandates respect for initial contacts and prohibits circumvention. The revised clause makes this respect conditional ('unless circumstances dictate otherwise') and permits circumvention 'at the discretion' of either party. This directly contradicts the original intent of preventing circumvention. This creates uncertainty within Clause 12, which details penalties for circumvention. If circumvention is permitted at a party's discretion, it is unclear when Clause 12's penalties apply, undermining the enforcement of the non-circumvention provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to circumv ent this agreement through the use of subterfuge involving the use of third parties. It is further un derstood that where any party contravenes the integr ity of this non‐circumvention p rovision, the contravening party shall: a.indemnify fully and pay over all commissions, fees or monies ob tained by the contravening party, directly or indirectly, to the party, directly or indirectly, to the party that was violated, and b.indemnify and to be liable for a ll legal fees for all parties, which may be incurred in the course of adjudication of said violation, and c.be subject to possible punitive a ction as awarded by the legal process. The signatories agree to keep confid ential the identity of any kind and all contacts so provided by any other signatories. Such identity including names, addresses, telephones, telefax n umbers, telex numbers or any ot her means, is the property of the introducing signatory and shall not be disclosed otherthan under the terms thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits circumvention. The changed text permits circumvention at a party's discretion. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines penalties for any party that circumvents the agreement. The changed text creates uncertainty regarding when the penalties for circumvention apply, leading to a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13 The Company agrees that, during the term of this agreement and for a period of 48 months thereafter, the Company shall not, whether by itself, its officers, personnel, contractors or agents of the Company or whether by a Company A f f i l i a t e o r t h e o f f i c e r s , p e r s o n n e l , c o n t r a c t o r s o r a g e n t s o f that Company Affiliates, solicit or seek to solicit orders from any customer of Fintricity for services similar to the services which Fintricity provides to t hat customer without the prior written consent of Fintricity.
perturbation.changed_text: 13 The Company agrees that, during the term of this agreement and for a period of 6 months thereafter, the Company shall not, whether by itself, its officers, personnel, contractors or agents of the Company or whether by a Company A f f i l i a t e o r t h e o f f i c e r s , p e r s o n n e l , c o n t r a c t o r s o r a g e n t s o f that Company Affiliates, solicit or seek to solicit orders from any customer of Fintricity for services similar to the services which Fintricity provides to t hat customer with the prior written consent of Fintricity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the time period that the non-solicitation is being held from 48 months to 6 months. The company agrees to not solicit orders for a period after the termination of the contract with Fintricity, and changing the time period creates uncertainty. This can affect Clause 17 which says 'The termination of this Agreement or the completion of the Business Purpose for any reason shall not affect t he obligations set out in this Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The termination of this Agreement or the completion of the Busi ness Purpose for any reason shall not affect t he obligations set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-solicitation period from 48 months to 6 months. This directly contradicts the original agreement, as clause 17 states that termination of the agreement does not affect the obligations set out in the agreement. Reducing the non-solicitation period weakens that obligation, and creates a direct and impactful contradiction.
